capacityDataQuality.k21Conclusion: 53 rooms; capacity=0 count is 0; no placeholders; capacity range 3-200; median 40
capacityDataQuality.k22gConclusion: All 53 rooms have real capacity (K21-FIX-A confirmed); range 3-200, median 40, avg 46
capacityDataQuality.thisAuditConclusion: Capacity is real: 53 rooms, range 3-200, median 40, no null/0/negative values.

taskStudentCountAudit.notes: All values computed via getTaskStudentCount algorithm (ClassGroup.studentCount, fallback 50). Duplicated class links are detected and deduped.

hc4Audit.notes: HC4 is implemented in both full and delta paths. Penalty is -1000. Triggered by getTaskStudentCount(studentCount) > room.capacity.

candidateStrategies.name: Large class prefers larger room (threshold-based)
candidateStrategies.description: Trigger only when taskStudentCount >= 50 (large class). If room.capacity < (taskStudentCount + buffer), apply a fixed penalty. Example: if taskStudentCount >= 50 and room.capacity < taskStudentCount * 1.2 → -3.
pros: Does not penalize small classes at all.
Easy to verify in harness (single threshold).
cons: Threshold (50) is hard-coded and may not match all schools.
Does nothing for small classes occupying huge rooms (waste).
Threshold tuning risk: may not fire often enough or may fire too often.
candidateStrategies.rationale: Reasonable but narrow. Does not address resource utilization (small class in huge room). Pair with Candidate C if used.

candidateStrategies.name: Capacity buffer / margin preference (utilization ratio)
candidateStrategies.description: Compute utilization = taskStudentCount / room.capacity. If utilization > 0.90, apply -2 (tight). Optional: if utilization < 0.30 and room.capacity >= 100, apply -1 (waste). Smooth, continuous penalty that scales with how tight or wasteful the match is.
pros: Naturally complements HC4 (which fires at utilization > 1.0).
Can penalize both tight and wasteful matches.
cons: Waste penalty may conflict with SC9 (room stability) when solver wants to keep the same room.
Penalties are uniform per slot; not a function of how tight (could be graduated).
candidateStrategies.rationale: Smooth, general, naturally aligned with HC4. Reuses getTaskStudentCount. Single helper shared by full + delta. Best balance of expressiveness and simplicity.

candidateStrategies.name: Avoid wasting large rooms for small classes
candidateStrategies.description: Trigger when taskStudentCount < 30 and room.capacity >= 100. Apply -1 (waste penalty). Complements Candidate A or B by addressing the small-class-in-huge-room case.
cons: May conflict with SC9 (room stability): solver may want to keep small class in same room for stability.
May conflict with HC1 (room conflict) solver logic: large room is often the only free one.
Wastes solver time on tiny penalty (-1).
candidateStrategies.rationale: Useful as a sub-bucket of Candidate B. Standalone use is too narrow. Better to combine waste-penalty into Candidate B (utilization < 0.30 branch).

candidateStrategies.name: Combined utilization band (tight + waste)
candidateStrategies.description: Single penalty based on utilization ratio: utilization > 0.90 → -2; utilization < 0.30 AND room.capacity >= 100 → -1; otherwise 0. Same as Candidate B with explicit waste branch.
pros: Single helper function, single penalty structure.
Both directions of utilization are covered.
Easy to test in harness (one formula, three branches).
cons: Slightly more complex than B alone (extra branch).
candidateStrategies.rationale: Recommended as the canonical SC10 design. Combines B (utilization > 0.90) and C (waste at utilization < 0.30 and capacity >= 100) into a single, testable rule.

skipRules: taskStudentCount <= 0 (no classes or all-zero counts — should not happen but defensive)
utilization > 1.0 (HC4 owns this case; SC10 skips to avoid double-counting)
recommendedDesign.penaltyScale: -2 for tight match (utilization > 0.90). Matches SC8 / SC9 base unit (per-gap / per-extra-room). -1 for waste (utilization < 0.30 with large room). Lighter than tight penalty. Both are within the existing soft penalty range (-1 to -20) and well below HC4 (-1000).

skipRules: taskStudentCount <= 0 (no classes or all-zero counts — should not happen but defensive)
utilization > 1.0 (HC4 owns this case; SC10 skips to avoid double-counting)
fullScoreDesign.penaltyScale: -2 for tight match (utilization > 0.90). Matches SC8 / SC9 base unit (per-gap / per-extra-room). -1 for waste (utilization < 0.30 with large room). Lighter than tight penalty. Both are within the existing soft penalty range (-1 to -20) and well below HC4 (-1000).

deltaScoreDesign.affectedKeyStrategy: Per-slot (moved slot is the only affected key). Unlike SC5 / SC8 / SC9, SC10 is NOT an aggregate constraint — it evaluates one slot at a time. Therefore the delta computation is O(1): re-evaluate SC10 on the moved slot at old and new positions.
deltaScoreDesign.complexity: O(1) per delta (just two utilization calculations)
deltaScoreDesign.minPertIsolation: Use 3rd-position originalAssignments (F3/F4/F6/F8 pattern) to isolate MIN_PERT in delta tests.

interactionAnalysis.notes: SC10 cannot weaken HC4. HC4 still triggers at utilization > 1.0. SC10 only fires when utilization <= 1.0. If a move brings a slot into utilization > 1.0, only HC4 penalty applies (deltaHard += -1000); SC10 does not add anything (skipped).

SC9 may keep a task in the same room across slots for stability. SC10 may want a different (more appropriately sized) room for a slot. Both are at -2 / -2. The LAHC solver will weigh them; if SC10 is wrong (penalizing a stable match), the bestScore will still converge to the global optimum. No hard conflict.
SC8 keys on (classGroup, day); SC10 keys on (slot, room). Different domains. No interaction.

Linxiao rooms tend to be small (capacity 25-92 in this dev.db). If automotive classes are placed in Linxiao per SC6 / HC6, they may have tight utilization (Linxiao rooms cluster at 25-40). SC6 (-20) is much stronger than SC10 (-1 or -2); solver will prefer Linxiao match over capacity. This is intended: specialty rule wins over utilization preference.

SC10 is independent of day. Capacity utilization is the same whether the slot is on a weekday or weekend. SC10 does not skip weekend slots (unlike SC5 / SC8 / SC9 which skip weekend). The reasoning: capacity preference is structural, not time-based.
SC10 does not depend on originalAssignments. It only cares about current assignment vs room capacity. Delta tests must still use 3rd-position originalAssignments to isolate MIN_PERT net 0.
Different key (room building vs room capacity). No interaction.
Different key (task-day vs slot-room). No interaction.
Different key (slotIndex vs room capacity). No interaction.
Different key (room building vs room capacity). No interaction.
Different key (teacher-day vs slot-room). No interaction.

harnessPlan.title: Good fit: utilization 0.50 → soft=0
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: teacherId=null (SC5 skip), 1 slot per task (SC2 skip), periods <5 (SC3 skip), weekday only (SC7 skip). Utilization 0.50 is in 0.30-0.90 band, no penalty.

harnessPlan.title: Tight fit: utilization 0.95 → soft=-2
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: Same isolation. Utilization > 0.90 fires the -2 tight branch.

harnessPlan.title: Over capacity: utilization 1.20 → hard=-1000, soft=0 (SC10 skips)
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: HC4 fires. SC10 skips (utilization > 1.0). Component assertion: SC10 details=0.

harnessPlan.title: Small class in huge room: utilization 0.20, cap=120 → soft=-1
Small class in normal room: utilization 0.40, cap=60 → soft=0
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: Utilization 0.40 is in 0.30-0.90 band, no penalty.

harnessPlan.title: room=0 (unscheduled): SC10 skip → soft=0
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: room=0 → SC10 skips. Total soft=0 (also HC1/HC4/HC5 skip on room=0).

harnessPlan.title: taskStudentCount=0 (no classes): SC10 skip → soft=0
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: Defensive skip. getTaskStudentCount returns 50 fallback for empty taskClasses, so this case requires manual fixture override.

harnessPlan.title: Boundary: utilization = 0.90 → soft=0 (strict >)
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: Boundary test: > 0.90 strictly. 0.90 itself does not fire.

harnessPlan.title: Move from tight (0.95) to good (0.50) → deltaSoft=+2
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: 3rd-position originalAssignments. Same task, different room. SC10 only.

harnessPlan.title: Move from good (0.50) to tight (0.95) → deltaSoft=-2
Move small class from huge (0.20, cap=120) to normal (0.50, cap=60) → deltaSoft=+1
Move small class from normal (0.50, cap=60) to huge (0.20, cap=120) → deltaSoft=-1
Move into over-capacity (utilization 1.10) → deltaHard=-1000, deltaSoft=0 (SC10 skips)
harnessPlan.isolationNotes: SC10 skips (utilization > 1.0). HC4 fires. Component assertion.

findings.title: Room.capacity is real: range 3-200, median 40, no null/0/negative
findings.currentStatus: All 53 rooms have real capacity. Min: 3, max: 200, median: 40, distinct: 23, default-50: 0, zero: 0, negative: 0. Confirms K21-FIX-A and K22-G conclusions.
evidence: Capacity distribution: [{"bucket":"<30","count":10,"percent":19,"notes":"small room / single-seat / lab"},{"bucket":"30-49","count":29,"percent":55,"notes":"small classroom"},{"bucket":"50-79","count":7,"percent":13,"notes":"standard classroom"},{"bucket":"80-119","count":6,"percent":11,"notes":"large classroom"},{"bucket":">=120","count":1,"percent":2,"notes":"lecture hall / auditorium"}]
Suspicious rooms: 5 (cap<10 or cap>150 — likely lab/lecture hall, not bug)
findings.risk: No risk. Capacity is real and ready for capacity-based soft preference.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with capacity-based soft preference design.

findings.title: Task student count distribution: min=2, max=483, median=31, 100% REAL_STUDENT_COUNT
findings.currentStatus: Tasks: 308. With classes: 308. Without classes: 0. Count source: {"REAL_STUDENT_COUNT":308,"FALLBACK_50_PER_CLASS":0,"MIXED":0}. ClassGroup with studentCount: 36/36. Duplicated class links: 0.
findings.risk: Low risk. Most tasks have real student counts. FALLBACK only applies to small subset.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with capacity-based soft preference; FALLBACK is acceptable for the minority of tasks.

findings.title: HC4: full + delta covered; 0 current violations in dev.db
findings.currentStatus: HC4 is implemented in both full and delta paths. Penalty is -1000. Trigger: taskStudentCount > room.capacity. Current state: 0 slots violate HC4. K22-C Harness B covers HC4.
evidence: K22-C Harness B covers HC4 invariant
findings.risk: No current HC4 violations. SC10 can build on top of a clean HC4 baseline.
findings.recommendation: SC10 must NOT weaken HC4. SC10 only fires at utilization <= 1.0. If a move brings utilization > 1.0, only HC4 penalty applies.

findings.title: SC10 design (Candidate D) is feasible: reuses getTaskStudentCount, no schema change
findings.currentStatus: Recommended design: SC10_ROOM_CAPACITY_UTILIZATION with utilization-based formula. Reuses existing getTaskStudentCount helper (capacity.ts). No schema change. No new data path. Full + delta share computeSC10Penalty(utilization, capacity) helper. Penalty scale -1 (waste) / -2 (tight), within existing soft range.
evidence: src/lib/scheduler/capacity.ts: getTaskStudentCount is the canonical helper
F4/F6/F8 pattern: shared helper for full + delta
findings.risk: Two thresholds (0.90 utilization, 0.30 utilization, 100 capacity) need calibration. Initial defaults are reasonable but should be tuned against real data.
findings.recommendation: Open K22-F11-CAPACITY-PREFERENCE-IMPL with Candidate D as the design baseline. Add Harness J with 13 cases (8 full + 5 delta).

findings.title: SC10 is orthogonal to SC1-SC5, SC7, SC8; soft-tradeoff with SC9; soft-priority with SC6/HC6
findings.currentStatus: Interaction analysis is complete. SC10 is per-slot (not aggregate), so it does not introduce any aggregate-key drift risk. Penalty scale (-1 to -2) is well below SC6 (-20) and SC9 (-2), so it can coexist without dominating.
evidence: see "Interaction analysis" section in markdown report
findings.risk: No risk. SC10 has clean interaction profile.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with implementation; no special handling required.

recommendedNextStage: K22-F11-CAPACITY-PREFERENCE-IMPL (recommended) OR K22-I-SCORE-WEIGHTS-AUDIT (if weight calibration risk is high) OR K22-G2-ROOM-TYPE-SCHEMA-PLAN (if room type is more important)
notes: K22-F10 is a read-only audit + design. No Prisma writes, no score.ts changes, no schema changes.
Capacity data is real (K21-FIX-A and K22-G confirmed).
Task student count is computable via existing getTaskStudentCount helper (capacity.ts).
HC4 is full + delta covered; no need to modify it. SC10 is purely additive soft preference.
SC10 design follows the F4/F6/F8 shared-helper pattern.
No new constraint is implemented in this stage. K22-F10 is audit-only.
